question:
Will South Africa take second place in Miss Universe 2025?

Description: The Miss Universe 2025 competition is scheduled to be held in Thailand on November 21, 2025. You can find more information about that here: https://www.missuniverse.com/the-competition-thailand/

This market will resolve according to the listed country represented by the contestant named first runner-up in this competition. 

If the result of this competition isn't known by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on official results as reported by the Miss Universe Organization.

evidences.title: Here's When & Where to Watch the 2025 Miss Universe Pageant in the U.S. - Remezcla
evidences.content: For audiences in the United States, the 2025 Miss Universe pageant will be available to stream via Telemundo and Peacock on Nov. 20. But you won’t have to get up early to watch it. Instead, the finals will take place at 8 a.m. in Thailand on Nov. 21. That means coverage will begin at 8 p.m. ET/ 7 p.m. CT/5 p.m. PT in the U.S. on Nov. 20. Preliminary coverage on Telemundo starts an hour before the show. The contest will be held at the exhibition and convention center Impact Challenger Hall in Pak Kret, Nonthaburi, Thailand. Carlos Adyan, Danilo Carrera, Jessica Carrillo, and Jacqueline Bracamonte will be the hosts. [...] And if you’re interested in the preliminary and National Costume Competitions, those will be airing Wednesday, November 19. They will be livestreamed on the Telemundo App, the @telemundoentretenimiento YouTube and Telemunfo Al Día FAST Channels. It will be at 1 p.m. Thailand time. That’s 1 a.m. ET. Related ### Miss Mexico Fátima Bosch Wins Miss Universe 2025 Miss Universe 2025 has been at the center of controversy this year, after Miss Mexico Fátima Bosch was called “dumb” by pageant executive Nawat Itsaragrisil during a pre-competition event that was streamed li...

evidences.title: Melissa Nayimuli crowned Miss Universe SA 2025, headed to finale in Thailand | News24
evidences.content: The news was met with widespread excitement as South Africans flocked to the post’s comment section to share their congratulations with the newly crowned beauty queen. One supporter wrote, “Finally, justice is being served. Melissa has been crowned Miss Universe South Africa!” Nayimuli shared in an official statement, “This moment is not just about me, it’s about every young girl who has ever dared to dream, even when the world told her it wasn’t her time. It’s about showing that perseverance, faith, and purpose always lead you exactly where you’re meant to be. Our voices matter, and when we speak with courage and conviction, the world listens.” [...] Nayimuli’s journey in pageantry started in 2020, when she entered the Miss South Africa competition but did not win. Undeterred, she returned in 2023 and became a top 5 finalist. ADVERTISEMENT > View this post on Instagram > > A post shared by Melissa Nayimuli (@melissanayimuli) In the same year, she also successfully produced her documentary Chosi-Chosi, further showcasing her creative talents. Looking ahead to her Miss Universe South Africa role, Nayimuli is committed to addressing critical societal issues, particularly xenophobi...

evidences.title: Miss Universe - Wikipedia
evidences.content: If for any reason the winner cannot fulfill her duties as Miss Universe, the first runner-up takes over. This protocol has happened only once as of 2024, when Panama's Justine Pasek succeeded Russia's Oxana Fedorova as Miss Universe in 2002 after Fedorova's termination that year. Aside from the winner and her runners-up, special awards are also given to the winners of the Best National Costume, Miss Photogenic, and Miss Congeniality. The Miss Congeniality award is chosen by the delegates themselves. In recent years, Miss Photogenic has been chosen by popular internet vote (she used to be chosen by media personnel covering the event), and the winning country for Best National Costume is announced after the naming of the semifinalists during finals night. Starting in 2024, Continental [...] Miss UniverseImage 5 Type International women's beauty pageant Parent organization JKN Legacy Inc. JKN Global Group Legacy Holding Group Headquarters Samut Prakan, Thailand New York City, United States First edition1952 Most recent edition2025 Current titleholderFátima Bosch Mexico Owners Jakkaphong Jakrajutatip Raul Rocha Cantú Vice Presidents Olivia Quido Language English Websitewww.missunive...

evidences.content: It wasn't until 1978 that an African woman, Margaret Gardiner from South Africa clinched the crown and became the new Miss Universe that year. Throughout history, the USA has produced the highest number of winners with nine Queens. Margaret Gardiner from South Africa was crowned the new Miss Universe in 1978 African beauty queens have also made a mark in the competition by first appearing in the maiden pageant held in 1952 and clinching the crown six times. evidences.title: Instagram
evidences.content: 🚨MISS SOUTH AFRICA ORGANIZATION WILL NOT SEND THEIR 2025 WINNER TO 74th MISS UNIVERSE ‼️ During a conference earlier today, the new leadership of Miss South Africa revealed that they will NOT be sending their 2025 winner to Miss Universe this year due to lack of time between the finals of Miss South Africa and the international competition. The Miss South Africa 2025 finals is set to be held on October 25, while the Miss Universe 2025 finals is on November 21, less than a month after. [...] This is the first time since 1998 that the Miss South Africa organization will not participate in Miss Universe. This organizations first winner who competed at Miss Universe was Kerishnie Naicker in 1998. Prior to 1998, South Africa’s representatives at Miss Universe qualified via other national pageants. At this moment, it is unsure whether South Africa will be fully absent from Miss Universe this year with Miss South Africa organization continuing next year, or a different organization will be rewarded the Miss Universe South Africa license. evidences.title: Miss Universe Press Release | Miss Universe
evidences.content: November 18, 2025 — New York, United States In light of recent public statements and social media posts, the Miss Universe Organization considers it necessary to clarify certain inaccuracies regarding the Beyond the Crown Program and the official judging processes of the Seventy Four Miss Universe competition. On 17 November, the Miss Universe Organization publicly released the official selection committee of the Beyond the Crown Program, an independent social impact initiative designed to promote leadership, service, education, health, inclusion, and meaningful support for charitable causes. This program operates entirely separately from the Miss Universe competition and from the official judging panel. She later worked in journalism and authored several books. 1978 – Margaret Gardiner (South Africa)© Carley Margolis ### 1977 – Janelle Commissiong (Trinidad & Tobago) Janelle Commissiong made history in Santo Domingo as the first woman of color to win Miss Universe.She used her platform to advocate for civil rights and racial equality. 1977 – Janelle Commissiong (Trinidad & Tobago)© CBS Photo Archive ### 1976 – Rina Messinger (Israel) Rina Messinger won in Hong Kong, becoming Israel’s first Miss Universe. She later served as a diplomat and public figure promoting international relations. evidences.title: Miss Universe 2025: Everything to Know and How to Watch
evidences.content: The 74th iteration of the annual Miss Universe contest will return this Friday, Nov. 21. Contestants will take the stage on Friday, representing well over 100 countries from across the globe as they vie for this year's crown. This year's pageant theme is "The Power of Love," which the Miss Universe Organization website describes as a thematic mechanism to bring together the 130 competing nations "in a shared vision of compassion, inclusivity, and strength." "Through this celebration, we honor love as a force that empowers, breaks barriers, and inspires positive change across the world," the website reads. [...] ## Related Stories 5 Miss Universe Rules You Didn't Know Contestants Have to Follow Image 5: The newly crowned Miss Universe 2024, Victoria Kjaer Theilvig (C) from Denmark, reacts as she celebrates amid the other participants after winning the 73rd edition of the Miss Universe pageant in Mexico City on November 16, 2024.:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc():format(webp)/miss-universe-rules-1-100925-cf23e59547fb443cb77385327f206784.jpg)Miss Universe Controversy Explained: What Happened to Miss Mexico at Pre-Pageant Event? evidences.title: Miss Universe - Pageant Planet
evidences.content: Each contestant is judged on her physical beauty, intelligence and personality in order to determine who will advance to the finals. [...] All contestants who meet the criteria are then accepted and invited to compete in their respective countries for the coveted title of Miss Universe. [...] The purpose of the Miss Universe pageant is to celebrate and honor the beauty, intelligence, and poise of females from all over the world. Every contestant strives to make a positive difference in her community and around the globe by promoting social causes that she is passionate about. In addition to celebrating beauty, the pageant serves as a platform for women to gain confidence and develop important life skills such as leadership, public speaking, and networking. Contestants also gain exposure through media coverage which helps them reach greater heights in their careers.
